GA4 Web Analytics B2C data product, providing market‑level performance metrics aggregated across web and app channels. It captures monthly and year‑to‑date (YTD) insights on user traffic, engagement, and e‑commerce behavior by geography, market, platform, device, channel, and time, enabling business users to evaluate digital performance and conversion effectiveness across markets.

10042128.text: Validate if the TRANSIENT table created has data.

values: It represents the month\'s end date. (YEAR_TO_DATE)
Source system primary key(made from concatination of the dimensional columns) of the table. (TABLE_KEY)
Total number of unique user sessions across the site or app. A session starts when a user engages and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity. (TOTAL_SESSIONS_MONTHLY)

An Engaged Session in GA4 is a session that meets any of the following criteria: 1. Lasts 10 seconds or more, OR 2. Has 1 or more conversion events, OR 3. Has 2 or more screen or pageviews (ENGAGED_SESSIONS_MONTHLY)
Total number of unique users who interacts with your website or app. (USERS_VISIT_MONTHLY)
The User Purchase Ratio shows the percentage of users who made a purchase out of all users in a given period. (USER_PURCHASE_RATIO_MONTHLY)

Engagement Rate is the percentage of sessions on your website that are considered \'engaged.\' A session is classified as engaged when a user: 1. Actively stays on the site for at least 10 seconds, or 2. Views 2 or more pages or screens, or 3. Triggers a conversion event (such as a purchase, sign-up, etc.) (ENGAGEMENT_RATE_MONTHLY)
Cart Conversion Rate is the percentage of  sessions where an item added to the cart ultimately results in a completed purchase. (CART_CONVERSION_RATE_MONTHLY)

Cart Abandonment Rate (also known as Shopping Cart Abandonment Rate) measures the percentage of sessions where users add one or more items to their shopping cart but leave the site or app without completing the purchase. (CART_ABANDONMENT_RATE_MONTHLY)
Total number of unique user sessions across the site or app. A session starts when a user engages and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity. (TOTAL_SESSIONS_YTD)

An Engaged Session in GA4 is a session that meets any of the following criteria 1. Lasts 10 seconds or more, OR 2. Has 1 or more conversion events, OR 3. Has 2 or more screen or pageviews (ENGAGED_SESSIONS_YTD)
Total number of unique users who interacts with your website or app. (USERS_VISIT_YTD)
The User Purchase Ratio shows the percentage of users who made a purchase out of all users in a given period. (USER_PURCHASE_RATIO_YTD)

Engagement Rate is the percentage of sessions on your website that are considered \'engaged.\' A session is classified as engaged when a user: 1. Actively stays on the site for at least 10 seconds, or 2. Views 2 or more pages or screens, or 3. Triggers a conversion event (such as a purchase, sign-up, etc.) (ENGAGEMENT_RATE_YTD)
Cart Conversion Rate is the percentage of  sessions where an item added to the cart ultimately results in a completed purchase. (CART_CONVERSION_RATE_YTD)

Cart Abandonment Rate (also known as Shopping Cart Abandonment Rate) measures the percentage of sessions where users add one or more items to their shopping cart but leave the site or app without completing the purchase. (CART_ABANDONMENT_RATE_YTD)
It Represents the timestamp when a record is created or last updated. (UPDATED_AT)

10042103.text: Validate if the TRANSIENT table created has data.

values: It represents the month\'s end date. (YEAR_TO_DATE)
Source system primary key (made from concatination of the dimensional columns) of the table. (TABLE_KEY)
Total number of unique user sessions across the site or app. A session starts when a user engages and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity. (TOTAL_SESSIONS_MONTHLY)

An Engaged Session in GA4 is a session that meets any of the following criteria 1. Lasts 10 seconds or more, O 2. Has 1 or more conversion events, O 3. Has 2 or more screen or pageviews (ENGAGED_SESSIONS_MONTHLY)
Total number of unique users who interacts with your website or app. (USERS_VISIT_MONTHLY)
The User Purchase Ratio shows the percentage of users who made a purchase out of all users in a given period. (USER_PURCHASE_RATIO_MONTHLY)

Engagement Rate is the percentage of sessions on your website that are considered \'engaged.\' A session is classified as engaged when a user 1. Actively stays on the site for at least 10 seconds, o 2. Views 2 or more pages or screens, o 3. Triggers a conversion event (such as a purchase, sign-up, etc.) (ENGAGEMENT_RATE_MONTHLY)
Cart Conversion Rate is the percentage of  sessions where an item added to the cart ultimately results in a completed purchase. (CART_CONVERSION_RATE_MONTHLY)

Cart Abandonment Rate (also known as Shopping Cart Abandonment Rate) measures the percentage of sessions where users add one or more items to their shopping cart but leave the site or app without completing the purchase. (CART_ABANDONMENT_RATE_MONTHLY)
Total number of unique user sessions across the site or app. A session starts when a user engages and ends after 30 minutes of inactivity. (TOTAL_SESSIONS_YTD)

An Engaged Session in GA4 is a session that meets any of the following criteria 1. Lasts 10 seconds or more, O 2. Has 1 or more conversion events, O 3. Has 2 or more screen or pageviews (ENGAGED_SESSIONS_YTD)
Total number of unique users who interacts with your website or app. (USERS_VISIT_YTD)
The User Purchase Ratio shows the percentage of users who made a purchase out of all users in a given period. (USER_PURCHASE_RATIO_YTD)

Engagement Rate is the percentage of sessions on your website that are considered \'engaged.\' A session is classified as engaged when a user 1. Actively stays on the site for at least 10 seconds, o 2. Views 2 or more pages or screens, o 3. Triggers a conversion event (such as a purchase, sign-up, etc.) (ENGAGEMENT_RATE_YTD)
Cart Conversion Rate is the percentage of  sessions where an item added to the cart ultimately results in a completed purchase. (CART_CONVERSION_RATE_YTD)

Cart Abandonment Rate (also known as Shopping Cart Abandonment Rate) measures the percentage of sessions where users add one or more items to their shopping cart but leave the site or app without completing the purchase. (CART_ABANDONMENT_RATE_YTD)
It Represents the timestamp when a record is created or last updated. (UPDATED_AT)

1.value:
-- Makes sure each BK only have 1 active flag = true
WITH VERSIONS_CNT AS (
    SELECT 
        DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL_KEY,
        COUNT(*) AS total_records,
        SUM(CASE WHEN ACTIVE_FLAG = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ACTIVE_FLAG_CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_SOCIALMEDIA."DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL"
    GROUP BY DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL_KEY
)
SELECT 'DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL: distinct BK cnt comparison active flag = true' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT
) as s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT
    WHERE ACTIVE_FLAG_CNT = 1
) as t

UNION ALL

-- Makes sure there is no record taht has VALID_FROM >= VALID_TO
SELECT 'DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL: VALID_FROM >= VALID_TO' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
	SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_SOCIALMEDIA."DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL"
) as s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_SOCIALMEDIA."DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL"
    WHERE VALID_FROM < VALID_TO
) as t

UNION ALL

-- Makes sure that the count distinct of the BK composite key in Staging == Presentation
SELECT 'DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL: distinct BK cnt comparison' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT SUB_CATEGORY) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_10_staging}."STG_GOOGLE_ANALYTICS"."T_GOOGLE_ANALYTICS_HACK_MONTHLY_MARKET"
) s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL_KEY) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_SOCIALMEDIA."DIM_CUSTOM_CHANNEL"
) t

1.value:
with EXCEPTION_MARKET_MAP AS (
    SELECT
        *
    FROM
        (
            VALUES
                ('South Cyprus', 'Cyprus', 'Cyprus'),
                ('North Cyprus', 'Turkey', 'Turkish Cyprus'),
                ('United States', 'USA', 'USA'),
                ('Bosnia', 'Bosnia-Herz.', 'Bosnia & Herz.'),
                ('United Arab Emirate', 'UAE', 'UAE'),
                ('Russia', 'Russian Fed.', 'Russia'),
                ('Curacao', 'Curaçao', 'Curacao'),
                (
                    'Trinidad And Tobago',
                    'Trinidad,Tobago',
                    'Trinidad and Tobago'
                ),
                (
                    'Dominican Republic',
                    'Dominican Rep.',
                    'Dominican Republic'
                ),
                ('Palestine', 'Palestine', 'Palestine Auth. Area'),
                ('Spain', 'Spain', 'Spain Mainland'),
                ('Slovakia', 'Slovakia', 'Slovak Republic'),
                ('South Korea', 'South Korea', 'Korea')
        ) AS t (
            SOURCE_MARKET_NAME,
            STD_COUNTRY_NAME,
            STD_MARKET_NAME
        )
)
select
    *
from
    exception_market_map
